Article Title: KN Panikkar, historian of ideas, passes away
Article Text: Noted Leftist historian K N Panikkar, who critiqued colonial historiography’s simplistic view of culture and highlighted how indigenous intellectuals offered an alternative paradigm of modernity, passed away at a private hospital in Thiruvananthapuram on Monday. He would have turned 90 next month.Panikkar, affectionately called KN by his colleagues, belonged to a select group of historians such as Bipan Chandra, Sabyasachi Bhattacharya and S Gopal who created a strong department of modern Indian history at JNU’s Centre for Historical Studies.His course on the history of ideas in India in the 19th century was pioneering. “Panikkar taught it at a time when history writing was dominated by economic and political concerns. He endeavoured, through his research, to claim a position of centrality for culture as an important ingredient both in human action and large political movements,” says modern India historian Salil Misra.Historian Rakesh Batabyal adds that Panikkar brought to attention how the ideas of Akshay Kumar Dutt, Lokahitawadi and other 19th century intellectuals needed no western validation: they were modern in their own right, and gave us a sense of the new world opening before us.“When he discussed the tradition of knowledge in Ayurveda, we began to understand the complexities of the Indian knowledge processes under colonialism,” he says.Modern India historian Aditya Mukherjee was both student and colleague of Panikkar. “He acquired the reputation of a great teacher when he taught in a college in Delhi University, and was invited to join JNU in 1972. He was a brilliant teacher who taught us social and religious reforms in the 19th century, making the subject come alive even to students like me who had no background in history, having graduated in economics,” says Mukherjee.Born in Guruvayoor, Panikkar left Kerala after completing his undergraduate studies and went on to earn his Master’s degree and doctorate from Rajasthan University, where he later married his college mate, (late) Usha Bhargava. He was also the founding vice-chancellor of Sree Sankaracharya University of Sanskrit, Kalady.The historian’s books include, “Against Lord and State: Religion and Peasant Uprising in Malabar”. “His role in presenting Malabar rebellion in its true historical context and highlighting its aspects as a freedom struggle, agrarian revolt and anti-imperialist movement is of historical significance”, noted Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan in his condolence message. “He was a proud Marxist yet he engaged with questions perceived as outside the mainstream of Marxist ideology, as seen in his important essay ‘Culture and Consciousness in Modern India,” says R Mahalakshmi, eminent historian of South India. Historian M G Sashibhooshan endorses the view. “I remember Panikkar accepting my request to present a paper on Pattanam at the History Congress, despite knowing that my opinion on Pattanam (Muziris) was different from that of Marxist historians”.Misra recalls Panikkar encouraging his students to disagree with him. “It was believed that he was more likely to reward dissenting ideas with higher grades, than those that simply conformed to his ideas. His classrooms became contestatory spaces in which he was not the only voice. Many other students offered their own ideas and he warmly endorsed all of them”.
Article Summary: Noted Leftist historian K N Panikkar passed away at a private hospital in Thiruvananthapuram on Monday. He belonged to a select group of historians such as Bipan Chandra, Sabyasachi Bhattacharya and S Gopal who created a strong department of modern Indian history.

Article Title: T20 World Cup 2026 Prize Money: How Much Will The Winner, Runners-Up, and semi-finalists Earn?
Article Text:
India's Axar Patel and teammates celebrate the wicket of New Zealand's Finn Allen (ANI Photo)

Extra trains and poojas: How India is preparing for T20 World Cup final

India national cricket team not only lifted the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 trophy but also secured a massive financial reward after defeating New Zealand national cricket team in the final in Ahmedabad.The champions will take home $3 million, which translates to approximately INR 27.48 crore, from the tournament’s prize pool. The runners up, New Zealand, will receive $1.6 million, around INR 14.65 crore.The International Cricket Council allocated the prize money across all teams participating in the tournament, with the total pool amounting to roughly INR 120 crore. The distribution is based on how far each team progressed in the competition.The two losing semi finalists, South Africa national cricket team and England national cricket team , will each receive $790,000, which is about INR 7.24 crore.Teams that reached the Super 8 stage but failed to qualify for the semi finals will earn $380,000 each, approximately INR 3.48 crore. Meanwhile, every team that participated in the group stage is guaranteed a payout of $250,000, equivalent to around INR 2.29 crore.India’s triumph in the final was built on a dominant performance with both bat and ball.Sanju Samson led the charge with a blistering 89 off 46 balls, helping India post a massive 255 for five in the summit clash at the Narendra Modi Stadium.In response, New Zealand were bowled out for 159 as India sealed a comfortable victory to become T20 World Cup champions. Jasprit Bumrah starred with four wickets for 15 runs, while Axar Patel picked up three wickets to complete a dominant performance.
Article Summary: Champions will take home $3 million, which translates to approximately INR 27.48 crore, from the tournament’s prize pool. The runners up, New Zealand, will receive $1.6 million, around INR 14.65 crore. The International Cricket Council allocated the prize money across all teams participating in the tournament.

Article Title: ICC Men’s T20 World Cup Winners List (2007–2026): Full list of tournament champions and runners-up
Article Text:
Photo/Agencies

Extra trains and poojas: How India is preparing for T20 World Cup final

Here's full list of T20 World Cup winners and runners up from 2007 to 2026

Year Winner Runner Up 2007 India Pakistan 2009 Pakistan Sri Lanka 2010 England Australia 2012 West Indies Sri Lanka 2014 Sri Lanka India 2016 West Indies England 2021 Australia New Zealand 2022 England Pakistan 2024 India South Africa 2026 India New Zealand

India defended their title in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup after defeating New Zealand by 96 runs in the final in Ahmedabad on Sunday, following a record total in the first innings.India posted 255 for five after strong contributions from Sanju Samson (89), Abhishek Sharma (52) and Ishan Kishan (54). The total is the highest ever scored in a T20 World Cup final. With the win, India also became the first side to defend the T20 World Cup title.Chasing 256, New Zealand lost early wickets while trying to increase the scoring rate.Finn Allen (9) and Glenn Phillips (5) were dismissed early, leaving the side under pressure.India’s bowlers then took control. Axar Patel returned figures of 3 for 27 and Jasprit Bumrah finished with 4 for 15.After the dismissal of Tim Seifert, who scored 52 from 26 balls, the result was largely decided.Earlier, after being asked to bat first, Samson and Abhishek attacked from the start against the New Zealand bowlers.The pair added 98 runs for the first wicket in 7.1 overs, laying the platform for a high total.India crossed the 200-run mark in the 15th over.New Zealand slowed the scoring when James Neesham dismissed Samson, Kishan and captain Suryakumar Yadav (0) within six deliveries in the 16th over.Hardik Pandya made 18 before getting out, while Shivam Dube remained unbeaten on 26 and scored 24 runs in the final over bowled by Neesham.India added 52 runs in the last five overs.
Article Summary: India beat New Zealand by 96 runs in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup final. India posted a record total of 255 for five in their first innings. The total is the highest ever scored in a T20 final. New Zealand lost early wickets while trying to increase the scoring rate.

Article Text: Soldier goes missing during training in J&K’s Rajouri
Article Summary: Soldier goes missing during training in J&K’s Rajouri. Soldier has been missing for more than 24 hours. Soldier was last seen in Rajouri, in the state's north- eastern Rajouri region. He was on duty with the Indian Army's Rapid Response Force.

Article Title: Houston Astros shortstop Carlos Correa celebrates son Kenzo turning three as Daniella Rodriguez shares adorable family moment
Article Text:
Carlos Correa and Daniella Rodriguez with their children (Image via: IG)

Carlos Correa and Daniella Rodriguez celebrate son Kenzo turning three with a cheerful dinosaur themed birthday moment

Carlos Correa enjoys a joyful family chapter as fatherhood brings new milestones off the field





Houston Astros shortstop Carlos Correa is used to bright stadium lights and high pressure games. But this time, the attention shifted to a joyful family celebration at home. His wife Daniella Rodriguez shared a cheerful Instagram post as their youngest son Kenzo turned three.The adorable photo quickly caught the attention of fans who enjoy seeing Correa’s life beyond baseball. In the image, the MLB star and Rodriguez lean toward their smiling toddler while celebrating the special moment. The picture reflects a warm and playful family scene.Rodriguez captioned the post with the message, “Our Kenzo is 3!!!” The photo soon received reactions from followers who loved the sweet family glimpse.For many fans, it was a heartwarming look at Correa’s life away from the field.The birthday celebration featured a fun dinosaur theme that immediately stood out. Kenzo sat on the decorated table dressed in a bright green dinosaur outfit. A “Happy Birthday” banner and green balloons shaped like the number three filled the background. The playful theme gave the celebration a cheerful and lively feel.Carlos Correa leaned toward his son while Daniella Rodriguez kissed him from the other side.Kenzo looked delighted as the moment unfolded. The simple family scene captured the joy of the birthday celebration.Their older son Kylo Daniel Correa stood beside his mother during the moment. He watched his younger brother as the family gathered around the decorated setup. The photo reflected a warm and relaxed family celebration. The moment highlighted the growing bond between the siblings.Correa and Rodriguez have built a close knit family since getting married in January 2020. The couple welcomed their first son Kylo Daniel Correa in November 2021. Their second son Kenzo Correa was born in March 2023.Rodriguez often shares family updates through social media. These posts frequently highlight birthdays, holidays, and everyday moments with their children. Many fans now follow Correa’s family updates as closely as his games. Photos of Kylo and Kenzo often appear on Daniella Rodriguez’s social media. The posts give followers a small window into their everyday life.Correa is still known for his strong presence with the Houston Astros. Over the years he has become one of the team’s most reliable players in the infield. His calm approach and experience have kept him an important part of the lineup.But moments like Kenzo’s birthday show a quieter side of the MLB star. Away from the field, Correa looks happiest spending time with his wife and children. For the family, celebrations like this have become special milestones.
Article Summary: Carlos Correa and Daniella Rodriguez celebrate son Kenzo turning three with a cheerful dinosaur themed birthday moment. Kenzo sat on the decorated table dressed in a bright green dinosaur outfit. A “Happy Birthday” banner and green balloons shaped like the number three filled the background.

Article Title: Vaishali Murder News: Speech-impaired teen beaten to death during T20 World Cup final over ‘wrong cheer’ after India wicket
Article Text:
In a tragic twist of fate, Kundan Kumar, a speech-impaired teenager in Bihar's Vaishali district, lost his life over a cricket match misunderstanding





PATNA: Even as the country was watching the epic class between India and New Zealand in ICC Men’s T20 World Cup final on Sunday evening, a tragic incident unfolded in Vaishali district, where a speech-impaired teenager was allegedly beaten to death following a misunderstanding during the match.The victim, 17-year-old Kundan Kumar, was allegedly assaulted in Jakkopur village under Mahnar police station area on Sunday evening while he was watching the match with other local youths on a cellphone.Kundan, son of farmer Brijkishor Rai and a resident of ward no. 18 of the village, was the youngest among four siblings. According to police, the accused has been identified as Kisan Kumar (17), son of Visunkant Thakur, who lives in the neighbourhood.Villagers said a group of teenagers had gathered to watch the final match on a cellphone when the incident occurred. During the game, when India lost a wicket, Kundan allegedly shouted. Another youth allegedly misinterpreted his reaction as a celebration of India losing the wicket.What began as a brief exchange soon escalated into a heated argument and then a physical fight.According to Kundan’s mother, the tragedy unfolded suddenly.“On March 8, Kundan went out of the house for some work. It is not known how he ended up near those people. Some boys from the village were watching the match on a cellphone. My son also started watching there,” she said.She alleged that one of the youths asked Kundan to move away but he stayed.“When India’s first wicket fell, Kundan screamed. They thought he was happy about the wicket falling. The youths beat him up and smashed his head,” she alleged.After the assault, locals rushed Kundan to Mahnar Community Health Centre (CHC), where he was given primary treatment. However, seeing his critical condition, doctors referred him to Hajipur Sadar Hospital, from where he was referred to Patna Medical College and Hospital (PMCH). But Kundan died on the way to Patna early Monday morning.Mahnar SDPO Praveen Kumar said, “The incident of assault took place in a mutual dispute between two youths over watching cricket. During the altercation, Kundan fell on a stone after being pushed, which caused him serious injuries. He was being taken to the PMCH for treatment but succumbed to his injuries on the way,” the SDPO said.Police said a forensic team visited the spot and collected samples from the crime spot. Two teams have been formed to track down the accused, and raids are underway to apprehend him.
Article Summary: Kundan Kumar, 17, was allegedly assaulted in Jakkopur village under Mahnar police station area. He was watching the match with other local youths on a cellphone. When India lost a wicket, Kundan allegedly shouted. Another youth misinterpreted his reaction as a celebration of India losing the wicket.

Article Title: Corey Seager celebrates wife Madisyn’s birthday with sweet message as he shares tender moment of her with baby Camden
Article Text:
Corey and Madisyn Seager (Image via: IG/X)

Corey Seager’s sweet birthday message for wife Madisyn reflects their long love story that began in high school





Texas Rangers shortstop Corey Seager recently gave fans a glimpse into his personal life while celebrating the birthday of his wife, Madisyn Seager. The MLB star shared a touching family photo on social media that quickly caught attention from followers online. The post offered a warm look at the couple’s life away from the baseball field.In the image, Madisyn is seen holding their baby boy while kissing him gently beside a peaceful lakeside setting. The quiet moment highlighted a loving family scene that fans rarely see during the busy baseball season.Seager paired the photo with a heartfelt birthday message for his wife while celebrating her role as a mother. The post quickly gained reactions from fans who enjoyed the family moment shared by the Rangers star. “Happy Birthday to Cam Cam's beautiful mommy! Here's to the best decade yet!! I mean 29 forever ”Madisyn also replied to Seager’s birthday post in the comments.“I love you and our sweet little Cam so much,” she wrote. Corey Seager and Madisyn Seager share a relationship that dates back to their teenage years. The couple first met while attending the same high school in North Carolina before starting their early romance.Like many young couples, their journey faced a pause when life took them in different directions. The two later reconnected and rebuilt the bond that first began during their school days.Their relationship also survived the challenge of distance for several years. Seager was building his MLB career while Madisyn continued her college studies in their home state. Seager proposed to Madisyn in December 2019 after four years of dating. The couple later married during the COVID-19 pandemic in a small ceremony surrounded by close family.Their wedding took place at Saddle Wood Farms in Murfreesboro, Tennessee near Nashville on December 5, 2020. The ceremony became even more special because Seager’s father officiated the event.The couple also added a playful touch during the ceremony. Their dog Hazel served as the ring bearer while wearing a custom tutu. Reflecting on the wedding, Seager once spoke about the emotional moment. “I’ll never forget how she looked walking down the aisle! Never," Corey told Inside Weddings of his wife.The couple welcomed their first child, Camden Miles Seager, on October 4, 2025. The arrival of their son marked a joyful milestone in their growing family.
Article Summary: Texas Rangers shortstop Corey Seager shared a touching family photo on social media. Madisyn Seager is seen holding their baby boy while kissing him gently. The couple welcomed their first child, Camden Miles Seager, on October 4, 2025.

Article Title: Shane Bieber’s wife Kara Maxine shares her 2026 goals with fans as one heartfelt line stands out during Instagram Q&A
Article Text:
Shane and Kara Bieber (Image via: IG/X)

Kara Bieber shares a glimpse of her personal goals with followers as a heartfelt line about being present for her son quietly stands out



(Image via: Kara Bieber's IG Story)

Kara Bieber also reflects on the Cleveland places she misses while interacting with followers online





Life away from the baseball field often gives fans a chance to see a more personal side of athletes and their families. That moment recently came when Kara Maxine Bieber, the wife of Toronto Blue Jays pitcher Shane Bieber, opened up to followers during an Instagram interaction. The post offered a thoughtful look at the personal goals she set for herself earlier this year.Through the Instagram Story, Kara shared part of a list she had written for herself. The goals reflected themes of faith, growth, and family life. Among them, one particular line quietly caught attention because of its heartfelt focus on motherhood.Kara responded to followers by sharing a screenshot of the goals she had written earlier.Instead of mentioning only one intention, she decided to reveal a few of the priorities she noted at the start of the year.Along with the screenshot, Kara explained the context behind the list she shared. She wrote, “I have a business section of this but here's a screenshot of the first five I wrote down beginning of the year.”The list included several goals connected to personal and spiritual growth. Among them were “Relationship with God is priority” and “Life coach / business mentor.”Another line reflected how she hopes to support the people closest to her. Kara wrote, “Continue to uplift my husband, family & friends - be their biggest cheerleaders.”One line from the list, however, stood out because of its emotional tone. Kara wrote, “Be my most present self with Kav.” The message referred to the couple’s young son, Kav McClain. The line highlighted her focus on motherhood and her desire to stay present for the moments she shares with her child.In another response shared on her Instagram Story, Kara spoke about Cleveland and the places that still come to mind when she thinks about the city. The location remains meaningful because of Shane Bieber’s earlier years pitching in Cleveland.Her answer carried a tone of nostalgia and appreciation for the community there. She wrote, “I miss my people & CLE - time to visit!” Kara also listed several local spots that she remembers fondly from their time in the area. The list included chattys pizza, mojos, thyme table, trust coffee, the green farm juicery, Heinens, Lakewood market, bar italia, luca west, juneberry table, sarita, and rocky river wine bar.For many Cleveland residents and baseball fans, the places she mentioned are well known local favorites. Her response suggested that the city still holds a special place in the couple’s memories. Shane Bieber and Kara Maxine married on January 21, 2023, in Malibu, California, after getting engaged in July 2021. The couple first met while studying at the University of California Santa Barbara.Their family expanded in March 2025 with the birth of their son, Kav McClain. Moments like Kara’s Instagram posts give followers a glimpse into how the couple balances baseball life, personal ambitions, and raising a young family together.
Article Summary: Kara Maxine Bieber, the wife of Toronto Blue Jays pitcher Shane Bieber, opened up to followers during an Instagram interaction. The post offered a thoughtful look at the personal goals she set for herself earlier this year. The goals reflected themes of faith, growth, and family life. One line from the list stood out because of its emotional tone.

Article Title: Mohammed Siraj's viral dig on his and Kuldeep Yadav's role in T20 World Cup triumph: 'Pani pilana, bat uthana'
Article Text:
Mohammed Siraj (Image credit: BCCI)

Ahmedabad erupts as Team India arrive at hotel after T20 World Cup win

Poll Who do you think was the standout player for India in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026? Sanju Samson Jasprit Bumrah





NEW DELHI: After India won the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 title, broadcasters went around collecting reactions from the players following the historic triumph. Among them was Mohammed Siraj , who is well known for his witty and humorous responses, and he delivered another memorable moment when asked about his emotions after the victory.India lifted their third T20 World Cup title — having previously won the tournament in 2007 under MS Dhoni , in 2024 under Rohit Sharma , and now in 2026 under the captaincy of Suryakumar Yadav .During the interaction, the presenter asked Siraj, “What will be the World Cup's line, I only believe in...?”To which Siraj replied, “Whole team. It was God's script as I was not in the team initially and then I got a surprise call and then we became the champions.”The presenter then asked: “Thoda sa frustrating ho sakta hai playing eleven se bahar rehna, lekin role kitna bada hai aap dono ka.” (It can be frustrating sometimes not being part of the playing XI, however, how big is your role?)Siraj responded with a humorous answer.“Haa bahut hi bada role hai hum dono ka, bahar baithke pani pilana back to back, bat uthana.” (Yes, we played a major role. Sitting outside and providing water to the players and holding their bats.)Standing beside him, Kuldeep Yadav burst into laughter.Siraj featured in just one match during the tournament, against the United States national cricket team, where he picked up three wickets for 29 runs. However, he did not play another game in the competition.Kuldeep also played only one match in the tournament — against Pakistan national cricket team — and took one wicket.“Net mein bowling. Fir humlog positive environment rakhte hai. Hamesha players ko aisa rehta hai ki khele, but team ka jo combination k liye humlog bahar baithe hai toh team is important. No person is important, but team is important. Toh humlog ka aisa tha ki team jo sochti hai wahi behtar hoga.” (Bowling in the nets. Then we keep a positive environment. Players always feel like they should play, but for the team combination if we are sitting outside, then the team is important. No person is important, but the team is important. So our thinking was that whatever the team thinks will be better), Siraj said.
Article Summary: India won the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 title. Mohammed Siraj and Kuldeep Yadav were asked about their emotions after the win. Siraj said it was 'God's script' as he was not in the team initially.

They were the overwhelming favourites coming into the tournament — a stunning run since winning the 2024 edition, playing in familiar home conditions and with almost all the players in form. But the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 did not pan out as expected for the defending champions and co-hosts India.Reaching the Super Eights was never going to be an issue, with the expanded 20-team tournament featuring several associate nations. But the real challenge was getting past the Super 8s and qualifying for the knockouts. And there, India were tested as they were hammered by South Africa in their first Super 8 match.After that, it was a must-win situation for India, especially in the final Super 8 clash against West Indies. It required some superb individual performances to carry the team forward.India were not completely in control even in the semi-final against England despite posting a record 253/7, eventually winning by just seven runs. Finally, riding on another blockbuster batting display in the summit clash, they thrashed New Zealand by a record 96 runs to defend the title.India rewrote T20 World Cup history with this win — becoming the first team to defend the title and the first to win it at home. They also became the first team to win the tournament three times, adding to their triumphs in 2007 and 2024.Despite it being a not-so-perfect campaign, Indian players somehow scraped through as they won their third ICC title on the trot — T20 World Cup 2024, Champions Trophy 2025 and T20 World Cup 2026.The India skipper started the campaign with an unbeaten 84 against USA in the tournament opener when the team was struggling at 77/6, but he did not have much success with the bat in the next eight matches, ending with a golden duck in the final.It was a topsy-turvy campaign as captain too, but he kept his wits intact, especially in the final, to guide India home. Surya, however, contributed consistently with the bat and finished with 242 runs - third most in the team - at an average of 30.25 and a strike rate of 136.72.Everything that could go wrong went wrong for the World No.1 T20I batter Abhishek Sharma.He started the tournament with a hat-trick of ducks. A 55 against Zimbabwe in the must-win Super 8 match gave some hope, but he struggled again against West Indies in the virtual quarter-final and England in the semi-final.Abhishek, however, repaid the team management’s faith by scoring a quick-fire 52 off 21 balls against New Zealand in the final.Undoubtedly one of the heroes of India's campaign, Sanju Samson made the difference in the title defence. Samson lost his place in the starting XI to Ishan Kishan before the tournament.He got just one game in the group stage before getting his opportunity in the Super 8s after India were hammered by South Africa.Samson then made the tournament his own with a stunning unbeaten 97 off 50 balls against West Indies in the virtual quarter-final. It was followed by two identical scores of 89 in the semi-final and final, joining the elite group featuring Shahid Afridi (2009) and Virat Kohli (2014) to achieve the feat.He ended the campaign as the third-highest run-getter with 321 runs and also hit the most sixes in the tournament — 24 — all in just five innings.It was one of the best comeback stories, as Ishan Kishan nudged Sanju Samson out of the opening slot alongside Abhishek Sharma before the tournament.When Abhishek was struggling for runs, it was Ishan who kept the scoreboard moving at the top, scoring 61 against Namibia and 77 against Pakistan in the group stage.He later lost the opening slot after Samson was promoted to open with India needing wins in the final four matches. But it did not deter him as he continued contributing at No.3, including 39 off 18 against England in the semi-final and 54 off 25 in the final against New Zealand.Ishan finished as India’s second-highest run-getter and fourth overall with 317 runs.India’s evergreen X-factor Hardik Pandya had another successful campaign, scoring quick runs, providing crucial breakthroughs and being a livewire in the field.Batting at No.5 or lower, Hardik scored two fifties and played his part in giving India strong finishes. He ended with 217 runs.With the ball, he provided timely breakthroughs and finished with nine wickets. His all-round performance against Namibia — 52 off 28 balls and 2 for 21 — earned him the Player of the Match award.India’s designated No.3 batter Tilak Varma had a below-par outing initially and often struggled to get going. The disappointing opening stands before Samson’s promotion did not help either.Except for a 16-ball 25 against USA, his strike rate remained a concern. In the next four innings at No.3, his strike rate stayed below 120, putting pressure on the middle order.However, once Samson moved to the top and Ishan batted at No.3, Tilak was pushed down the order and found his rhythm. His unbeaten 44 off 16 against Zimbabwe, 27 off 15 against West Indies and 21 off seven against England in the semi-final helped India reach the final.Shivam Dube played the middle-overs enforcer’s role effectively. He consistently scored quick runs to give India strong finishes.His 66 off 31 balls against Netherlands, along with figures of 2/35, helped India remain unbeaten in the group stage. Dube was run out three times while attempting quick runs, reflecting his aggressive intent.Although he picked up five wickets with his medium pace, it was his batting that stood out. His unbeaten 26 off just eight balls played a key role in taking India’s total in the final to 255/5, well beyond New Zealand’s reach.Vice-captain Axar Patel was a livewire in the field, proving the phrase “catches win matches” — particularly in the semi-final against England, where his fielding efforts broke crucial partnerships.He also picked up important wickets, including three in the final, and finished with 11 wickets in seven matches.India’s pace spearhead Jasprit Bumrah once again proved why he is head and shoulder above the rest.He picked wickets in almost every match and delivered an excellent spell of 3/15 against South Africa too despite India’s big defeat. But his defining performance came in the final against New Zealand.Defending a massive target, Bumrah produced a sensational spell of 4 for 15 — the best figures by a pacer in a T20 World Cup final.He finished as the joint-highest wicket-taker of the tournament with 14 wickets, alongside Varun Chakaravarthy, while maintaining a superb economy rate of 6.21.The World No.1 T20I bowler began the tournament strongly, claiming nine wickets in the group stage, including 2/17 against Pakistan.However, his form dipped in the Super 8 stage. He was hammered for 47 runs by South Africa and struggled to regain control.His toughest outing came in the semi-final against England, where he conceded a record 64 runs in four overs.After the group stage, he returned figures of five wickets for 225 runs in five games — a stark contrast to his group-stage performance of nine wickets for 62 runs. Still, his early exploits ensured he finished joint-highest wicket-taker with Bumrah with 14 wickets.Arshdeep Singh was not at his wicket-taking best. The leading wicket-taker in T20Is for India returned nine wickets from eight innings and went wicketless in the final.His best figures were 3 for 24 against Zimbabwe, while his most expensive outing came against England in the high-scoring semi-final.Overall, it was a decent campaign, though overshadowed by Bumrah’s brilliance.India’s designated finisher Rinku Singh had a disappointing campaign and eventually lost his place when Sanju Samson was drafted in at the top.His final outing was a two-ball duck against South Africa in the first Super 8 match.Rinku finished with just 24 runs from five innings at a strike rate of 82.75 — well below expectations for a finisher’s role.Washington Sundar played just two matches and remained wicketless in both — 0/36 in four overs against Netherlands and 0/17 in two overs against South Africa.He lost his place after Axar Patel returned to the playing XI.With the team management persisting with Varun Chakaravarthy, Kuldeep Yadav featured in just one match — against Pakistan.He returned figures of 1 for 14 in three overs. It was difficult to judge his performance from a single outing in a long tournament, and it was unfortunate he did not get more opportunities.Mohammed Siraj also played just one match — India’s tournament opener against the USA.He made an immediate impact, returning figures of 3 for 29 in four overs and finishing as the highest wicket-taker in the match.However, the return of Jasprit Bumrah in the next game meant Siraj had to sit out due to team combination.
Article Summary: India won the T20 World Cup 2026 in South Africa. Suryakumar Yadav was named man of the match. Abhishek Sharma and Tilak Varma were among India's best performers. India beat New Zealand and Pakistan to win the tournament.

Article Title: Shoaib Akhtar slams India after historic T20 World Cup win: 'India have ruined cricket'
Article Text:
Shoaib Akhtar. (Twitter Photo)

T20 World Cup final: Fans in Ahmedabad go wild | India create history

Poll What played the biggest role in India’s ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 victory? Strong Team Strategy Individual Player Performances





NEW DELHi: India’s commanding win in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 final has sparked strong reactions worldwide, especially from neighbours Pakistan. Former fast bowler Shoaib Akhtar controversially said that India’s dominance has “ruined cricket. ”India dominated New Zealand in the final at Narendra Modi Stadium, winning by 96 runs to claim their third T20 World Cup title. While most cricket experts praised India’s flawless performance, Shoaib Akhtar criticised the team on the Tapmad show Game On Hai, saying India was like a “rich kid” dominating a neighborhood game.“It’s like when there’s one rich kid in a neighbourhood who calls all the poor kids and says, ‘Come, let’s play cricket.’That’s exactly what India is doing with us. Out of eight teams, they keep four, and out of those four they call three again and move forward, and then they say, ‘See, I’ve won.’ They have ruined cricket entirely,” Akhtar said.Interestingly, Akhtar had spoken differently before the final. While calling India the favourites, he said he hoped New Zealand would win “for cricket’s sake.”“I feel New Zealand might be bogged down in front of India, but India also have the pressure of 1.5 billion people.They have already lost a match in the World Cup final to Australia in Ahmedabad. I see India as the winner out of all this, but for cricket's sake, it's high time New Zealand won this World Cup,” he had said earlier.Despite his criticism, Akhtar admitted one reason for India’s success. He praised coach Gautam Gambhir for supporting players during tough times, saying that this kind of trust can make a big difference in major tournaments.India made cricket history on Sunday by beating New Zealand by 96 runs at Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. They became the first team to defend the Men’s T20 World Cup title, the first to win three T20 World Cups, and the first to lift the trophy on home soil.
Article Summary: India dominated New Zealand in the final at Narendra Modi Stadium, winning by 96 runs to claim their third T20 World Cup title. Shoaib Akhtar criticised the team on the Tapmad show Game On Hai, saying India was like a “rich kid” dominating a neighborhood game.

Article Title: ICC breaks silence on bias claims after England exit leaves West Indies, South Africa stuck in India
Article Text:
South Africa and West Indies cricket team (Agency Image)

Proud families celebrate India’s World Cup heroes | SKY, Abhishek & Axar’s loved ones react

Poll What should be prioritized in future international tournaments to avoid travel issues? Better Travel Planning Flexible Scheduling

Michael Vaughan post





The International Cricket Council has responded to allegations of bias after travel complications following the ICC Men's T20 World Cup sparked a major controversy involving several teams.Although the tournament concluded on Sunday with India national cricket team defeating New Zealand national cricket team in a one-sided final at Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad, some participating teams have remained stuck in India due to ongoing airspace restrictions linked to the conflict in the Middle East and West Asia.Among those affected are the West Indies national cricket team and the South Africa national cricket team. The West Indies played their final match of the tournament on March 1 but are still in India, while South Africa have also been unable to leave after their semi-final defeat to New Zealand on March 4.In contrast, the England national cricket team returned home shortly after losing their semi-final to India on March 5. The difference in travel timelines has triggered criticism, with many questioning how England managed to depart while other teams remained stuck.One of the most outspoken voices has been former England captain Michael Vaughan, who accused the ICC of favouring certain teams."All teams in this situation should be treated the same. Just because you are more powerful at the ICC table shouldn't count," he said.According to a report by BBC, the ICC has strongly denied any suggestion of preferential treatment and clarified that logistical factors alone determined when teams could leave."Decisions on departure sequencing are being driven solely by airspace availability, aircraft routing permissions, visa requirements and operational safety considerations."Any suggestion that teams have been prioritised based on anything other than these constraints is incorrect," BBC quoted an ICC spokesperson.The situation had already drawn attention earlier when West Indies head coach Daren Sammy expressed his frustration on X. In one post he wrote, "I just wanna go home", before sharing additional messages reflecting the team’s growing impatience.Subsequent reports indicated that arrangements had been made for a chartered flight to take the Caribbean side home, with the team expected to depart later today. South Africa’s squad is also likely to leave India today.The ICC also explained why England were able to travel earlier than the other teams."For instance, the England team and their staff were able to fly out from Mumbai without restriction due to the route's airspace being unaffected and flights operating as usual."The ICC categorically refutes any claims of bias in this or other instances, and has been fully accommodating of specific demands and conditions put forth by teams," it said.
Article Summary: The International Cricket Council has responded to allegations of bias after travel complications following the ICC Men's T20 World Cup. Some participating teams have remained stuck in India due to ongoing airspace restrictions linked to the conflict in the Middle East and West Asia. Among those affected are the West Indies national cricket team and the South Africa national cricketteam.

Article Title: Shahid Afridi Ex-Pakistan captain lauds Samson, Bumrah for outstanding T20 World Cup show
Article Text:
Team India Celebrating T20 World Cup Win and Shahid Afridi

India’s T20 World Cup win sparks celebrations | Rajeev Shukla, Tharoor & others react





Legendary all-rounder and former Pakistan captain Shahid Afridi praised India for becoming the first team in history to defend the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup title. In a video on X, Afridi said India deserved the win after they crushed New Zealand by 96 runs on Sunday, March 8, to lift the 2026 T20 World Cup.“The World Cup is over. I think India deserved the title. They have a great team combination. The players sitting on the bench were also as good as the playing XI. This is why I say that your bench needs to be strong. India’s bench was very strong; they could have picked anyone from there and slotted them into the playing XI. They played the whole tournament as champions," Afridi said in a video on his X account.According to Afridi, Bumrah is one of the best bowlers, who has carried India on his back for a long time with his impressive performances.Afridi also praised the performances of Sanju Samson, Abhishek Sharma, and Ishan Kishan for their important contributions to India’s win.“Sanju Samson was outstanding. He got the opportunity and he delivered with his sensible hitting and his ability to build the innings. Abhishek Sharma also performed well tonight. Ishan Kishan also performed outstandingly, just like he had performed against Pakistan. Jasprit Bumrah was the backbone of their bowling who has carried India on his back for a long time with his impressive performances.Be it with the new ball, old ball, slower one or yorker, he is one of the best bowlers in my opinion," he added.India made history by winning the 2026 ICC Men’s T20 World Cup, successfully defending their title and becoming the first team ever to retain the trophy. They also became the first country to win the Men’s T20 World Cup on home soil. With this victory, India has now won the T20 World Cup three times, in 2007, 2024, and 2026, the most by any team in the tournament’s history.
Article Summary: India became the first team in history to defend the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup title. Shahid Afridi said India deserved the win after they crushed New Zealand by 96 runs on Sunday, March 8, to lift the 2026 World Cup.

Article Title: Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos’ rumored prenup dispute sparks new debate after Micah Parsons’ brother weighs in
Article Text:
Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos (Image Via Getty)

Terrence Parsons Jr. questions Sarah Jane Ramos as Dak Prescott breakup rumors continue





Dallas Cowboys quarterback Dak Prescott is suddenly in the news for reasons that have nothing to do with football. Reports earlier this week claimed that Prescott and his fiancée, Sarah Jane Ramos, have ended their relationship just weeks before their planned wedding in Italy. The couple share two young daughters, so the unexpected development quickly caught attention across NFL circles and social media. According to reports from TMZ and People, the breakup came shortly after their joint bachelor and bachelorette celebrations in the Bahamas.The wedding had been scheduled for April. Neither Prescott nor Ramos has publicly confirmed the split, but the rumors quickly spread online. As the story gained traction, another voice jumped into the conversation. Terrence Parsons Jr., the brother of NFL linebacker Micah Parsons, shared his thoughts on social media. His short comment about prenuptial agreements quickly fueled more discussion around the rumored reason behind the breakup.Terrence Parsons Jr., the brother of Green Bay Packers linebacker Micah Parsons, reacted to the rumors on social media with a pointed message. He wrote, “If it’s really love a prenup should be no problem right.?”The post appeared to reference speculation circulating online that a disagreement about a prenuptial agreement may have caused tension between Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos. However, multiple reports say there is no confirmed proof that a prenup dispute caused the breakup, and the exact reason behind the split remains unclear.Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos first became publicly linked in 2023. Their relationship moved quickly. The Dallas Cowboys star proposed in October 2024 after signing a four-year, $240 million contract extension with the franchise.The couple later welcomed two daughters together. Their first child, Margaret Jane “MJ” Rose Prescott, was born in February 2024. Their second daughter, Aurora Rayne Prescott, arrived in May 2025.Reports say Ramos ultimately called off the wedding following a disagreement during the couple’s pre-wedding trip in the Bahamas. After the split, the two are expected to focus on raising their daughters together as co-parents.While the personal news grabbed headlines, Prescott’s professional situation is also drawing attention. The Cowboys recently restructured his contract to create salary cap space ahead of the upcoming NFL season.Dallas has missed the playoffs in each of the last two seasons. Because of that, Prescott will face heavy expectations when the 2026 season begins. The quarterback remains one of the most important players in the franchise’s future, and the team hopes he can guide them back into the postseason.
Article Summary: Dak Prescott and his fiancée, Sarah Jane Ramos, split just weeks before their planned wedding in Italy. The couple share two young daughters, so the unexpected development quickly caught attention across NFL circles and social media. Terrence Parsons Jr., the brother of NFL linebacker Micah Parsons, shared his thoughts about prenuptial agreements.

Article Title: How to pronounce Alysa Liu’s name? Olympic gold medalist Alysa Liu finally reveals correct way after Milano Cortina Winter Olympics fame
Article Text:
How to pronounce Alysa Liu’s name? Olympic gold medalist Alysa Liu finally reveals correct way after Milano Cortina Winter Olympics fame (Image via Getty)

Alysa Liu explains correct pronunciation of her name after defeating Kaori Sakamoto and Ami Nakai at Milano Cortina Winter Olympics





Alysa Liu has been one of the most talked-about athletes after her big moment at the Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics. The 20-year-old American figure skater won two gold medals and suddenly found herself everywhere, on TV shows, social media, and in sports headlines around the world. But while people were celebrating her Olympic success, there was one small detail almost everyone kept getting wrong: how to say her name. In a recent interview with Newsweek, Alysa Liu shared that most fans and even media outlets have been pronouncing her last name incorrectly for years. She explained that while she does not get upset about it, the real pronunciation is actually different from what many people say.Alysa Liu made history at the Milano-Cortina Games by becoming the first American woman in 24 years to win the Olympic gold medal in women’s singles figure skating. Her strong performances helped Team USA win the team event as well. Along the way, she defeated top Japanese skaters Kaori Sakamoto and Ami Nakai, which made her victory even more memorable. Now, as fans continue to celebrate her Olympic success, Alysa Liu has decided to finally explain the correct way to say her name.During the interview with Newsweek, Alysa Liu calmly explained how her name should really sound.“Technically, this is how my family says my name: ‘Ah-Lee-sa Lee-Oh,’” Liu said.However, she also admitted that people around the world usually say it differently.“‘Ah-Li-sa’ is how my friends call me. And that’s how the world calls me,” she said.The biggest confusion comes with her last name. Many people say “Lee-Ooh” or even “Lu.” Liu said she understands why people get confused and does not expect everyone to say it perfectly.“And then my last name, a lot of people call me ‘Lee-Ooh’ or ‘Lu,’” Liu explained.“I personally don’t care. It can be hard to pronounce. I don’t expect people to know how to say that. But technically my name is ‘Ah-Lee-Sa Lee-Oh.’”While Liu has been enjoying the spotlight after the Olympics, she also had an uncomfortable moment soon after returning home. She shared the experience on Instagram, explaining that a group of fans crowded her at the airport.“So I land at the airport, and there’s a crowd waiting at the exit with cameras and things for me to sign,” she wrote.“All up in my personal space. Someone chased me to my car bruh. Please do not do that to me.”Despite the excitement around her Olympic success, Liu has decided not to compete in the upcoming World Figure Skating Championships scheduled in Prague later this month. Her name is no longer listed on the official website of the International Skating Union, though no reason for the withdrawal has been confirmed.Earlier, actor Daniel Radcliffe even praised Liu during her appearance on The Today Show, showing how widely her Olympic performance has been noticed. For now, Alysa Liu remains one of the biggest stars to come out of the Milano-Cortina Games, and fans finally know how to say her name correctly.
Article Summary: Alysa Liu became the first American woman in 24 years to win the Olympic gold medal in women’s singles figure skating at the Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics. The 20-year-old American also helped Team USA win the team event as well. Liu has decided not to compete in the World Figure Skating Championships scheduled in Prague later this month.

Article Title: Olympic gold next target for Suryakumar Yadav
Article Text:
Suryakumar Yadav (ANI Photo)

T20 World Cup: Gautam Gambhir, Suryakumar Yadav after Team India win





AHMEDABAD: Having led India to a T20 World Cup title, Suryakumar Yadav is now targetting the 2028 LA Olympics. “The next goal is the Olympics — Olympic gold. In 2025 I had a tough year. I couldn’t get a fifty for a long time. But ups and downs are part of the game. You have to respect the game and be true to yourself. Article Title: Preity Zinta celebrates from stands as India defend T20 World Cup title: 'None of us could keep calm'
Article Text:
Preity Zinta at the stadium during the T20 World Cup final. (Video grabs)

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

Proud families celebrate India’s World Cup heroes | SKY, Abhishek & Axar’s loved ones react

Poll Who do you think had the biggest impact on India’s win? Jasprit Bumrah Sanju Samson





NEW DELHI: India’s historic triumph in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup final sparked a wave of celebration across the country, and Bollywood stars were among the loudest cheerleaders. Actor Preity Zinta , who watched the match live from the stands, shared her excitement on social media after India’s dominant victory over New Zealand in Ahmedabad.The actor was present at the Narendra Modi Stadium on Sunday and later posted pictures and videos capturing the electrifying atmosphere during the final. Her Instagram update featured glimpses of her cheering enthusiastically along with friends, including singer Iulia Vantur, as India marched towards a memorable win.Reflecting on the experience, Zinta wrote that watching a World Cup final live is always special, especially when the home side lifts the trophy. “It’s always special to see a World Cup final live in the stadium & be on the winning side. What a game, what a team & what a feeling seeing #TeamIndia win their 3rd ICC Men’s T20 World Cup title. Of course none of us could keep calm,” she posted, celebrating the emphatic victory.Zinta arrived at the stadium in a simple white T-shirt paired with blue jeans before proudly switching into the Indian jersey during the match, fully embracing the celebratory mood inside the packed arena.The celebrations extended beyond the stadium as Bollywood’s biggest names flooded social media with congratulatory messages. Among them was superstar Shah Rukh Khan , who hailed the Indian side for their commanding performance.“What a comprehensive victory!! Well done to our men in blue!!! You guys are champions and watching you on the field has made us all very proud. Take a bow. Jai Hind!” he wrote on X, echoing the sentiment of millions of fans across the country.Several other celebrities—including Akshay Kumar, Kareena Kapoor, Karan Johar, Anushka Sharma, Sunny Deol and Anil Kapoor—also joined the online celebrations.On the field, India produced a commanding display with the bat as Abhishek Sharma, Sanju Samson and Ishan Kishan led the charge with the top order. Their collective effort powered India to a huge total before the bowlers completed a comprehensive 96-run win over New Zealand.With the victory, India scripted history by becoming the first nation to successfully defend the T20 World Cup title.
Article Summary: India beat New Zealand by 96 runs to win the ICC Men's T20 World Cup final. India are the first nation to successfully defend the title. Actor Preity Zinta watched the match live from the stands. Shah Rukh Khan and Akshay Kumar among Bollywood stars to congratulate India.

Article Title: Gautam Gambhir on why individual records shouldn’t define Indian cricket: ‘Milestones don’t matter, trophies do’
Article Text:
Gautam Gambhir (Getty Images)

T20 World Cup: Gautam Gambhir, Suryakumar Yadav after Team India win

‘High-risk, high-reward’

‘Social media doesn’t matter’

Tribute to Dravid, Laxman





After all, his team had just crushed New Zealand by 96 runs in the most one-sided T20 World Cup final ever. Even amid the celebrations, though, Gambhir made it clear what he believes should shape Indian cricket during his tenure. Individual milestones, he said, will never take precedence over team success as long as he is in charge.“My simple philosophy with Surya has always been that milestones don’t matter. Trophies matter,” Gambhir said. “For too long in Indian cricket we’ve spoken about milestones. I hope that till I’m here, we’re not going to talk about milestones. Stop celebrating milestones and celebrate trophies.”India’s emphatic win made them the first team to win three T20 World Cups (2007, 2024, 2026), the first to defend the title and the first host nation to lift the trophy. Reinforcing his point, Gambhir cited Sanju Samson ’s fearless batting in the knockout games. “You saw what Sanju did in the last three matches.Imagine, if he was playing for milestones maybe we wouldn’t have got to 250. That’s the mindset needed in team sport.”Gambhir said India’s approach in the tournament was simple: attack from ball one to ball 120. “The plan was simple — high risk, high reward,” he said. “If you start fearing defeat, you will never win. I told the captain we won’t play for scores like 160-170. I’d rather accept getting bowled out for 100 because totals of 150-160 take you nowhere in T20 cricket.”The philosophy was evident as India smashed 250-plus totals in both the semifinal and the final.Gambhir also dismissed outside noise, insisting his accountability lies only with the players in the dressing room. “My accountability is not towards social media. It is towards those 30 people sitting in the dressing room. Even if I win two ICC trophies as a coach, that doesn’t matter. Those 30 people matter the most during my tenure.”The former opener, who became the first Indian to win the T20 World Cup both as player and coach, dedicated the triumph to Rahul Dravid and VVS Laxman. “What Rahul bhai did for Indian cricket during his tenure deserves huge respect. And VVS Laxman has done tremendous work behind the scenes at the Centre of Excellence, which remains the pipeline for our cricket.”Gambhir admitted he had little head-coaching experience before taking over the role, having previously mentored IPL sides Lucknow Super Giants and Kolkata Knight Riders. “When I was given this job, I didn’t have experience as a head coach. For the BCCI to trust me with this responsibility means a lot,” he said.
Article Summary: Gautam Gambhir says milestones will never take precedence over team success. India beat New Zealand by 96 runs in the T20 World Cup final on Sunday. India are the first team to win three World Cups and the first to defend the title. The former opener dedicated the triumph to Rahul Dravid and VVS Laxman.

Article Title: Jarryd Dale Error: Why Team Australia missed World Baseball Classic quarterfinals after South Korea win as controversial tiebreaker rules
Article Text:
Why Team Australia missed World Baseball Classic quarterfinals after South Korea win as controversial tiebreaker rules (Image via Getty Images)

Jarryd Dale error and Do Yeong Kim play explain how South Korea won the Pool C tiebreaker over Australia





South Korea and Australia played a tense World Baseball Classic game on Monday, and the result changed everything in Pool C. South Korea defeated Australia 7-2, which helped them reach the World Baseball Classic quarterfinals in Miami. But many fans were confused. Australia had already won two games in the pool stage, so people wondered why they were knocked out. The answer lies in the World Baseball Classic tiebreaker rules. After the game ended, South Korea, Australia, and Chinese Taipei all finished with the same 2-2 record in Pool C.When teams have identical records, the tournament does not simply look at total wins. Instead, it uses a set of statistical tiebreaker rules to decide which team advances. Because of those rules, South Korea moved on, while Australia and Chinese Taipei were eliminated. The game itself also had a dramatic moment late in the match that changed the final score and ultimately sealed Australia’s fate. According to tournament information shared by World Baseball Classic officials, the final run margin and defensive mistakes in the late innings played a huge role in determining who advanced.Before the game started, Australia actually had the best chance among the three teams. They entered the matchup with a 2-1 record, while South Korea was also 2-1 and Chinese Taipei had already finished 2-2. That meant the final game between Australia and South Korea would decide everything.In the simplest terms, Australia needed either a win or a smaller losing margin to qualify. If they beat South Korea, they would move directly to the quarterfinals. Even if they lost, some scorelines could still allow them to advance depending on the tiebreaker calculations.However, the 7-2 result favored South Korea, allowing them to win the tiebreaker among the three teams. The game itself was very tight at one point. South Korea built an early 5-0 lead, but Australia fought back. In the fifth inning, Robbie Glendinning hit a solo home run that reduced the gap. Later in the eighth inning, Travis Bazzana added an RBI single that made the score 6-2 and kept Australia within reach.Then came the moment that Australian fans will remember for a long time. With runners on base, Do Yeong Kim was walked and replaced by the fast Hae-Min Park as a pinch runner. South Korea captain Jung Hoo Lee hit a weak ground ball to Australian shortstop Jarryd Dale.It looked like a routine play. Dale only needed to throw the ball to second baseman Travis Bazzana to catch Park. But the throw went wrong. Park safely reached third base instead of being out. That mistake proved costly.Soon after, Hyun Min Ahn, the KBO Rookie of the Year, hit a sacrifice fly that allowed Park to score. The run pushed the score to 7-2, which gave South Korea the exact margin they needed to win the tiebreaker.Because of that final score, South Korea advanced to the quarterfinals in Miami, where they are expected to face the winner of Pool D, likely the Dominican Republic or Venezuela, according to tournament coverage from World Baseball Classic updates.Meanwhile, despite winning two games earlier in the tournament, Australia’s campaign ended in the pool stage.
Article Summary: South Korea defeated Australia 7-2 in a World Baseball Classic tiebreaker on Monday. Australia had already won two games in the pool stage, so people wondered why they were knocked out. The answer lies in the tournament's tiebreaker rules, which use a set of statistical rules to decide which team advances.

Article Title: Andretti’s Jake Dennis and fiancee Lexi Grace Boosey share glimpses of their Marbella home under construction
Article Text:
Jake Dennis and Lexi Grace Boosey are overseeing work at their new house in Spain's Marbella. (Image credit: Instagram)

Jake Dennis and fiancee Lexi Grace Boosey share updates from their new house construction



Jake Dennis and Lexi Grace Boosey's new house is under construction. (Image credit: Instagram)

Jake Dennis and Lexi Grace Boosey are overseeing work at their new house. (Image credit: Instagram)

Jake Dennis proposed to girlfriend Lexi Grace Boosey after Sao Paulo win







Jake Dennis is making full use of the long break after the Jeddah E-Prix before the Andretti driver heads to Madrid for the Formula E Round 6 race. Both he and his fiancee, Lexi Grace Boosey, have been looking after the construction of their new house and have shared glimpses of their new abode on Instagram. The two got engaged in December after Dennis won the first race of the season in Sao Paulo. It seems the couple is planning to get hitched soon after completing their new house.Jake Dennis took to Instagram Stories to share a glimpse of his fiancee, Lexi Grace Boosey, at the site. She is seen in a beige overcoat, on top of a ladder as she takes measurements to add some storage over a shelf. He Marbella, Spain as the location in the picture. Marbella is a upscale resort city on Spain’s Costa del Sol in Andalusia.But it was Boosey who shared many more glimpses of their house on her Instagram Stories. She showed a sitting area, the fireplace spot, and the television area in the living room, with work in progress at the site. She also shared a picture of the mood board, which reflected their vision for the upholstery and other interior design plans for the place.Jake Dennis began the 2026 Formula E season by bagging victory at the Sao Paulo E-Prix in early December. Days later, he announced his engagement to longtime girlfriend Lexi Grace Boosey. Sharing a picture of her showing off the huge diamond on her ring finger, he simply wrote: “Long term contract signed!”The couple recently came back from a group vacation in the French Alps, where they often go. They had earlier visited the place with their dog, Billy.Dennis recently signed a multi-year contract extension with Andretti after being a part of the team for over five years. He joined Andretti as his first Formula E team and continues to be a part of it. He made the announcement on Instagram: “A new multi year contract signed with Andretti! It’s hard to believe it’s been 5 years since I first joined the team! Can’t wait to continue our journey together and take our success into the Formula E Gen 4 era!”Dennis still has time to oversee the new house before he heads to Spain for the maiden Madrid E-Prix. It is scheduled for March 20-21 and will be followed by a Rookie Test for aspiring car racers a day later.Also read: Oliver Rowland fulfills promise to cheer for protege Arvid Lindblad at Australian GP, flies from Dubai to Melbourne amid Middle East tension
Article Summary: Formula E driver Jake Dennis and fiancee Lexi Grace Boosey have shared pictures of their new house in Marbella, Spain. The couple got engaged in December after Dennis won the first race of the season in Sao Paulo. Dennis recently signed a multi-year contract extension with Andretti.

Article Title: Did Rashan Gary’s goodbye post hint at a Packers trade? “Hacked” claim surfaces after viral message
Article Text:
Rashan Gary (Image Via Getty)

Rashan Gary’s social media post and Packers salary cap situation spark speculation





Green Bay Packers pass rusher Rashan Gary surprised fans on Friday when a goodbye message suddenly appeared on his social media accounts. The post, shared on Instagram and X, looked like a farewell note to the team and its supporters. Naturally, fans quickly started wondering if the defensive star was leaving the Packers before the 2026 NFL season. However, the situation soon took another turn. According to reports from Packers beat writers, the posts were later described as the result of Gary’s accounts being hacked.That explanation came after the message had already spread widely across social media. The timing of the situation also drew attention. The Packers had just managed to move under the NFL salary cap earlier this week, which means the team now has flexibility in how it handles several roster decisions this offseason. Because of that context, the unexpected message from Gary quickly became a major talking point among fans and NFL reporters.The message posted on Rashan Gary’s accounts sounded very much like a farewell to Green Bay. In the statement, the pass rusher reflected on the moment he joined the team in 2019 and thanked fans for their support.Gary wrote, “When I got the call in 2019, it was one of the greatest moments of my life, and it always will be. The opportunity to represent Green Bay and Packers Nation across the country and around the world is something I will never forget.”He continued by saying, “Like all chapters in life, this one has come to an end. I want to thank everyone who supported me and my family throughout the years. The love, the energy, and the memories mean more than words can explain.”The message ended with a confident note about his future. Gary added, “On to my next stop — nowhere near done yet. All 6’5”, 275… can’t wait.”Soon after the post went viral, several Packers reporters said the team’s understanding was that Gary’s accounts had been hacked. That explanation raised eyebrows for some observers because the post appeared on multiple platforms and even included a graphic.Meanwhile, the football side of the situation remains unchanged. Rashan Gary is still under contract with the Packers for the 2026 season and is scheduled to earn about $18 million in salary.According to ESPN reporter Adam Schefter, no official move has been made regarding Gary’s future. Schefter wrote, “Packers edge Rashan Gary has not been released at this time. No definitive action has been taken. All options still remain on the table, including a potential trade.”The Packers recently restructured safety Xavier McKinney’s contract, a move that helped the team get under the salary cap before the new league year begins on March 11. Because of that move, Green Bay does not need to make immediate roster decisions.General manager Brian Gutekunst has also said that players currently under contract for 2026 are expected to return for the season. For now, Gary remains part of that group while the team evaluates its next steps during the offseason.
Article Summary: Rashan Gary’s social media post and Packers salary cap situation spark speculation. The post, shared on Instagram and X, looked like a farewell note to the team and its supporters. The Packers had just managed to move under the NFL salary cap earlier this week.

Article Title: Lucas Di Grassi: Lucas Di Grassi calls for FIA modernisation after backlash to new F1 rules: “I want to see pure performance”
Article Text:
Lucas Di Grassi (Image credit: Instagram)

Lucas Di Grassi calls for FIA overhaul amid backlash to new F1 rules

What Lucas Di Grassi wants from the FIA





Formula E veteran Lucas Di Grassi has given his verdict on the new F1 rules as the Australian Grand Prix had several drivers including Max Verstappen and Lando Norris expressed their disappointment over the new F1 cars. The Lola Yamaha ABT driver has shortlisted the underlying issues he feels exist in the FIA and has called for a more modern approach to multiple formats to keep each format unique and different from one another.Lucas Di Grassi shared a video on Instagram and captioned it: “The sport could do better. We always have to strive for excellence.# #f1 #formula1 #fia #rules.”The Formula E veteran said in the video, “Just because I've seen so many negative comments about the new Formula 1 rules… The rules are decided by the FIA, both in sporting and technical terms. And you have smart and intelligent people within the FIA. But the way the structure is organized, the way the rules are decided, they aren't always aligned with the best interests of the promotional party. Parties like Formula One, Formula E, or WEC.“There's a bit of a disconnect between what the FIA decides and what the FIA wants, and what's needed for the sport, in my opinion, especially on the promotional side. Also, because it's a federation, there's no FIA ownership, so they shy away from a number of innovations and a number of different approaches and incentives that could be put in place to accelerate that innovation faster or to choose the right path, rules, or the technical and sporting rights, so that the sport could thrive in the right way.So you end up with some discrepancies, such as, for example, with the rules that F1 has now in 2026, or for example, it took more than seven, eight years to get involved in Formula 8 traffic in four-by-four,” he added.Further sharing what he wants to see from the FIA, Lucas Di Grassi said, “So there are many examples that I believe if they were better organized and if the instances and structure within the FIA were a little more modern, we would have achieved this much more quickly. Including making the championships, the feeder series and the lower-level championships, cheaper and more accessible. So, I hope we can change these structures, modernize the FIA, make better decisions that influence the sport in a much, much better way. ”Confirming his vision for F1 and other formats, he concluded, “And… Yes, I would like to see Formula 1 with pure performance, I would like to see Formula E with pure efficiency, I would like to see the WEC with road-relevant technologies, very discreet, different technologies, working side by side, but not cannibalizing each other, not with many innovations that used to happen in the past, when there was more freedom. Anyway, there are many things to do, but I hope this will be for the best of our sport.”Reacting to Di Grassi’s video, a fan wrote: “I've been thinking priorities are kind of twisted lately. Looks like number one peers to please are the investors, then the manufacturers, then the sponsors and lastly the audience.” Many others expressed they agreed with his point of view.Di Grassi still has ten days to go for the Madrid E-Prix, lined up from March 20-21. Its a new venue on the Formula E calender and a Rookie Test will also follow the E-Prix on March 22.Also read: Oliver Rowland fulfills promise to cheer for protege Arvid Lindblad at Australian GP, flies from Dubai to Melbourne amid Middle East tension
Article Summary: Lucas Di Grassi calls for FIA overhaul amid backlash to new F1 rules. Lola Yamaha ABT driver has shortlisted the underlying issues he feels exist in the FIA. He has called for a more modern approach to multiple formats to keep each format unique.

Article Title: Top 5 richest tennis players in 2026: Roger Federer leads billion-dollar list with Serena Williams, Novak Djokovic and Rafael Nadal
Article Text:
Who are the richest tennis players in 2026? Find out.Image via: Getty

Roger Federer – Estimated net worth: $1.1 billion approximately



Serena Williams – Estimated net worth: $300-350 million approximately



Novak Djokovic – Estimated net worth: $240-250 million approximately



Rafael Nadal – Estimated net worth: $220 million approximately



Andy Murray – Estimated net worth: $160 million approximately







Tennis has always had a certain glamour to it. But the money flowing through the sport today is on a completely different level compared to even a decade ago. Grand Slam prize funds keep climbing, tournaments are paying out more than ever, and the biggest stars are signing sponsorship deals that can sometimes make their actual prize money look like small change. For the players who made it to the very top, the real wealth rarely came from tennis alone. It came from what they built around it. Smart brand partnerships, well-timed investments, and personal business ventures have helped a small group of players turn a great career into a genuinely life-changing fortune. Here are the five richest tennis players in 2026.Roger Federer is simply at the top when it comes to wealth. He earned more than $130 million in prize money during his playing career, which is a remarkable number on its own. But that is not what made him a billionaire. It was the empire he has built off the court. Rolex, Mercedes-Benz, Uniqlo — his endorsement list reads like a luxury brand directory. And then came his investment in Swiss sportswear company On, which turned out to be one of the shrewdest moves any athlete has ever made. Federer is the first tennis player in history to cross the billion-dollar mark according to Forbes, and it is hard to imagine anyone catching him any time soon.Serena Williams is widely considered the richest female tennis player who has ever lived, and the numbers back that up. She brought in nearly $95 million in prize money across her career according to Celebrity Net Worth, along with a collection of titles that will never be forgotten. But Serena has always had her eye on something bigger than tennis. Through Serena Ventures, she has backed dozens of companies across different industries. Add in her fashion work and her global endorsements, and her wealth has kept growing well after her playing days wound down.No player in tennis history has earned more prize money than Novak Djokovic. His total from tournaments alone sits at more than $186 million, a record that may stand for a very long time. Off the court, he has kept things equally busy. Partnerships with Lacoste, Head and Hublot have been consistent earners, and he has also put money into restaurants, wellness projects and other ventures that quietly keep adding to the total.Rafael Nadal built something rare — a career so respected that the brand essentially sold itself. His long partnerships with Nike and Kia brought in serious money alongside everything he earned on the court. But perhaps the most personal part of his financial story is the Rafa Nadal Academy in Mallorca, a project he clearly cares deeply about and one that has grown into one of the most respected training centres anywhere in the sport.Andy Murray tends to be the quietest name on this list, but his financial story is genuinely interesting. Three Grand Slam titles, solid endorsement deals with Under Armour and Jaguar, and then a pivot into technology startups and sports businesses that not many people saw coming. Murray has never been someone who courts attention off the court, but he has been building something real and it shows in the numbers.Most of these players have already stepped back from competing regularly, but their presence in the sport is still very much felt. And with prize money continuing to rise and sponsorship deals getting bigger every year, the current generation of players may eventually have a real shot at matching what these five built over their careers.
Article Summary: Roger Federer is the first tennis player in history to cross the billion-dollar mark. Serena Williams is widely considered the richest female tennis player who has ever lived. Novak Djokovic has earned more prize money than any other player in tennis history.

Article Title: Rory Mcilroy: Rory McIlroy's net worth vs $200 million brand contract: How the brand deal boosted the golfer's net worth
Article Text:
Rory McIlroy's net worth vs $200 million brand contract? How the brand deal boosted the golfer's net worth

Rory McIlroy's $200 million Nike contract



Rory McIlroy’s Rejected LIV Golf Payday





Rory McIlroy is a Northern Ireland-born professional golfer. Rory McIlroy has been the world's top-ranked professional golfer for more than 100 weeks. Rory is a four-time major champion, winning the 2011 U.S. Open, 2012 PGA Championship, 2014 Open Championship, and 2014 Ryder Cup. Rory McIlroy's estimated net worth, according to Celebrity Net Worth , is $250 million.Rory is among the highest-paid athletes on the planet; he easily earns $40-50 million from tournament winnings but mostly from endorsements, including from brands like Nike, TaylorMade, and so on.Rory McIlroy, one of the most marketable athletes in the world for a long time, has attracted lucrative endorsement deals from international brands. In 2013, he reportedly signed a blockbuster endorsement deal with Nike valued between $100 million and $250 million.In 2017, that relationship was formalized and extended with a 10-year, $200 million contract. However, after Nike left the golf equipment business, McIlroy secured a $100 million contract with TaylorMade in 2017 to exclusively utilize its clubs, balls, and golf bag.Beyond Nike and TaylorMade, McIlroy has endorsed a wide range of other businesses in luxury goods, tech, finance, and clothing and apparel.He signed with Omega Watches as a brand ambassador and has appeared prominently in their international marketing campaigns.He partnered with JPMorgan Chase in the financial sector, particularly on the Chase Sapphire card brand. He has also promoted NBC Sports' GolfPass, a subscription service he helped develop, which combines golf instruction, tee-time booking, and streaming video. He has also received endorsements from UnitedHealth Group, Bose, and EA Sports, for which he was the cover athlete for the Rory McIlroy PGA Tour.Rory McIlroy has amassed over $104 million in official PGA Tour prize money, making him the second-highest earner in PGA Tour history after Tiger Woods. Rory McIlroy was one of the most expressive critics of LIV Golf and repeatedly declined offers to join the Saudi-backed league, despite top players making the move for massive paydays.According to multiple reports, McIlroy was reportedly offered a deal worth between $300 million and $500 million to leave the PGA Tour. The figures would have instantly placed him among the highest-paid athletes in history. There were rumors that he rejected a deal that included equity in LIV Golf plus a massive payday that brought the total value of the offer to $850+ million. He denied these claims. Despite tempting sums, McIlroy chose to stay loyal to the PGA Tour, citing moral concerns about the source of LIV's funding and his faith in the legacy and competitive integrity of the traditional tour.
Article Summary: Rory McIlroy's estimated net worth, according to Celebrity Net Worth, is $250 million. He earns $40-50 million from tournament winnings but mostly from endorsements, including from brands like Nike, TaylorMade, and so on. He has also received endorsements from UnitedHealth Group, Bose, and EA Sports.
